tldr: Matt, 66 live ads are leaning hard on one brand-name headline and a handful of recycled body lines across UK/FR/DE/IT — at least 18 ads will hit fatigue inside 14 days, with CPMs likely climbing 20-35% by mid-May. The Leo Gregory tee cluster and 'Out with the old. In with the new.' drop are already at terminal velocity.

benchmark.context: Streetwear category median is ~22 days to fatigue; Weekend Offender is sitting at ~12 because 41 of 66 ads use the bare 'Weekend Offender' headline and the same 6-7 body lines are translated rather than re-conceived. Localization is being mistaken for variation.

ads.headline: default_collection_headline
ads.body: Weekend Offender: Fresh tones and statement graphics designed with sharp focus on functionality and detail.
drivers: Headline is literally the placeholder 'default_collection_headline' — Meta is serving an unfilled template variable, which kills CTR and signals zero creative QA
Body copy is duplicated verbatim across indexes 0, 1, 10, 13, 28 with only language swaps
Same Leo Gregory tee PDP landing as 4 other ads — auction overlap is cannibalizing itself

ads.headline: default_collection_headline
ads.body: Weekend Offender: Fresh tones and statement graphics designed with sharp focus on functionality and detail.
drivers: Headline 'default_collection_headline' is a broken Shopify variable — this should never have shipped and is actively burning impressions
Identical Fresh tones / statement graphics body running in 6 languages across the account
Variant-level PDP link guarantees thin reach inside an already-saturated Leo Gregory tee cluster

ads.headline: Weekend Offender's Best
ads.body: The Overnighter. A fan favourite for a reason. Crafted from high-density weave 100% polyester with a compact, cabin-ready design that fits aeroplane hand luggage. Built tough. Travels light. Explore our bestsellers.
drivers: Exact same body copy as indexes 4 and 8 — three near-identical Overnighter ads competing in the same UK auction
Headline 'Weekend Offender's Best' is repeated three times with no angle differentiation
All three point to /collections/bestsellers with the same UTM term — Meta will throttle the lowest performer fast

ads.headline: Weekend Offender
ads.body: Out with the old. In with the new. Weekend Offender's new drop is here.
drivers: 'Out with the old. In with the new.' runs in EN (6, 37, 58), DE (11, 18), FR (14, 23, 31) — 8 clones across the pool
Generic 'Weekend Offender' headline shared with 40+ other ads, zero hook differentiation in feed
Same /collections/new-in destination as 9 other live ads — placement diversity is gone

ads.headline: Leo Gregory Graphic T-Shirt White - XL / White
ads.body: Were you looking at this? If you weren't, you should be.
drivers: Retargeting hook 'Were you looking at this? If you weren't, you should be.' duplicated on indexes 15 and 59
SKU-with-size headline format ('XL / White') reads like a feed export, not a hook
Leo Gregory tee has 9 separate ads in the pool — cookie-pool overlap is severe

ads_compact.headline: Weekend Offender
ads_compact.body: The Technician Jacket with mesh-lining, water-repellent coating and built-in face mask for protection against the elements.

ads_compact.headline: Weekend Offender
ads_compact.body: Not all trainers are equal. Karhu. Diadora. Saucony. WO x Veras. See the difference. Now available at Weekend Offender.

ads_compact.headline: New spring '26 collection.
ads_compact.body: New silhouettes. Fresh palette with softer tones and considered contrasts.

hero_concept.headline: Grohl Over-Top. Cyclone Grey.
hero_concept.primary_text: Cut for the walk to the ground, the after-party, and the train home. The Grohl Over-Top in Cyclone Grey Camo — heavyweight shell, oversized fit, pulled straight from the terrace playbook. Music. Rave. Terrace. Film. Designed in the UK. New drop, no apologies.
hero_concept.visual_direction: Hero shot of the Grohl Over-Top Cyclone Grey Camo on a concrete plinth against a graffiti-tagged wall, single-product focus with editorial framing.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Across all 66 ads there's no single-product hero shot tied to a specific named SKU with a culture-led hook — everything is either a generic 'Weekend Offender' brand line, a recycled translated body, or a thin Leo Gregory tee variant. This concept finally pairs a named hero piece with the brand's strongest existing voice ('Music. Rave. Terrace. Film.') in the format Matt's pool is missing.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a raw concrete underpass setting with a tagged graffiti wall behind and a low matte-grey plinth presenting the garment, with directional cool overcast daylight raking across the fabric to reveal the camo texture and weight of the shell. Moody British streetwear aesthetic — desaturated cyclone-grey palette, subtle film grain, contemporary terrace-culture mood, premium fashion editorial styling reminiscent of Weekend Offender's UK design language. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. Maintain the exact appearance, color, and details of the product from the reference image. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no text, no logos, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no buttons, no faces, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products.

next_step.urgency: 5 ads die inside 9 days. The 'default_collection_headline' bug is bleeding now.
next_step.headline: Book 30 min — let's ship the Grohl hero before next weekend
next_step.body: Matt, I'll walk you through the auction overlap on the Leo Gregory cluster and the Overnighter triplet, then we line up the Grohl Over-Top hero plus 2 backups so you've got fresh creative live before mid-May CPM creep. Quick, specific, no fluff.

method_note: Scores derived from pattern detection across all 66 live ads (headline/body/CTA/landing repetition + launch clustering); directional, not a substitute for in-platform frequency and CPM data.
